props.message:
Stoneroost.

The Source thins here. Khorgaul has hoarded the warmth — the pines stand like bones, the moss clings cold. Climb if you can.
A switchback bench. The stone is scored where countless feet have turned. Glim's wisp pales in the wind: the climb steepens.

An old marker, half-buried.

"Lirien wove the high places first, that the Source might see far. The hoarder builds nests; the Maker built sight."

props.open_message: A small iron key, etched with the Triglyph. Heavier than it looks.
A hookshot. The chain is cold; the spike is colder. It longs for high places.
A handful of river-smooth pebbles. The hoarder forgot these.
From the summit, the plain unfolds in mist. The chest holds twelve perfect stones — a gift from the wind.

root.speaker: Hermit Cael
root.text: You climbed. Few do. Sit a moment. Breathe the thinness. The Source thins here — but what thins also clarifies. Speak.

peak.speaker: Hermit Cael
peak.text: Stoneroost. The crow-stones perch here, and so do I. From this height the realm reads like a single page — Hearthold's smoke, the Brookhold green, the dark over the Hollow. Look long enough, and you can read the wound.

source.speaker: Hermit Cael
source.text: Below, it sings; here, it whispers; in the Hollow, it screams. All three voices are the Source. Few learn to hear all three in one ear.

khor.speaker: Hermit Cael
khor.text: Khorgaul does not destroy. He gathers. He hoards. He keeps. The Source thinned at Stoneroost not by Khorgaul's malice but by his patience. Patience is a slow and terrible weapon.

self.speaker: Hermit Cael
self.text: I came here young, and the wind has worn me to a quiet thing. I taught the wind to speak, some say. The wind taught me to listen, I would answer.

wind.speaker: Hermit Cael
wind.text:
Listen — *holds up a finger*
The wind moves over the stone. It carries the names the Hoarder forgets. If you ever lose your own name, climb. The wind keeps a copy.

bye.speaker: Hermit Cael
bye.text: Down is faster than up. Mind the loose stones.

root.speaker: Warden Briga
root.text: Halt — or rather, mind your step. I keep the south trail. There are bone-knights in the gullies and bats in the high cuts. You'll want to know which is which before you meet either.

bats.speaker: Warden Briga
bats.text: Bone-bats — small, fast, drop on you from above. A pebble or an arrow takes them down before they bite. Don't bother with the sword unless they grounded.

knights.speaker: Warden Briga
knights.text: Bone-knights — heavy, patient, will not yield. Strike when their guard drops, then back away. They are not living, but they are not careless. Treat them like a moving wall with a sword.

other.speaker: Warden Briga
other.text: The hermit at the summit knows things he won't say plain. If he gives you a riddle, write it down. If he gives you silence, that's an answer too.

bye.speaker: Warden Briga
bye.text: Up the trail, then. Mind the gullies.

props.warp_message: The high stones learn a name. The Roost is open to you.

root.speaker: Wind-worn Stone
root.text: *A faint hum, like wind through stone…*

hint_pre.speaker: Wind-worn Stone
hint_pre.text: Reach for the perch. The Roost waits in the wind, beyond the leap.

hint_post.speaker: Wind-worn Stone
hint_post.text: You can reach where the Roost roosts. Rise.
